---
title: "High-Frequency Tracker: Calendar Heatmap"
author: "Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    code_folding: hide
params:
  # Master Data Path
  data_path: "../1_data/master_daily_interaction.csv"
  
  # Variables
  date_var: "date"
  value_var: "adoption_rate"
  
  # Policy-Facing Labels
  metric_name: "Daily Adoption Rate (%)"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.path = "../4_output/figures/",
  fig.width = 10,
  fig.height = 3.5, 
  fig.retina = 3
)
```

```{r load-and-prep}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)

# Load data
raw_data <- read_csv(params$data_path)

# ==============================================================================
# DATA SCRUBBING & PROCESSING BLOCK
# ==============================================================================
plot_data <- raw_data %>%
  # 1. Safely drop rows missing the actual date string
  drop_na(!!sym(params$date_var)) %>%
  
  # 2. Standardize Date and Numeric Value
  mutate(
    Date = as.Date(!!sym(params$date_var)),
    Value = as.numeric(!!sym(params$value_var))
  ) %>%
  
  # 3. Missing Day Protection: Ensure every single day exists in the sequence
  # so the calendar grid doesn't collapse if there's a gap in the data.
  complete(Date = seq.Date(min(Date, na.rm = TRUE), max(Date, na.rm = TRUE), by = "day")) %>%
  
  # 4. Mathematical Calendar Generation
  mutate(
    # Extract structural components
    Year = year(Date),
    Month = month(Date, label = TRUE, abbr = FALSE),
    # Start the week on Monday (1)
    DayOfWeek = wday(Date, label = TRUE, abbr = TRUE, week_start = 1),
    
    # Calculate the exact week of the month for grid positioning
    first_day_of_month = floor_date(Date, "month"),
    first_day_wday = wday(first_day_of_month, week_start = 1),
    WeekOfMonth = ceiling((day(Date) + first_day_wday - 1) / 7),
    
    # Reverse the factor so Monday plots at the top of the Y-axis
    DayOfWeek = fct_rev(DayOfWeek)
  )
```

## Daily Interaction & Adoption

```{r plot-heatmap}
source("../2_R/2.0_setup_theme.R", local = TRUE)

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = WeekOfMonth, y = DayOfWeek, fill = Value)) +
  
  # Draw the calendar squares with a thin white border to separate them
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  
  # Split the chart into individual months (Removed scales = "free_x" to avoid aspect ratio crashes)
  facet_wrap(~ Month, nrow = 1) +
  
  # Ensure the squares stay perfectly square (GitHub style)
  coord_equal() +
  
  # Use a continuous color gradient: Light Grey (Low) to CERP Primary (High)
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "#EBEBEB", 
    high = unname(cerp_cols["primary"]),
    na.value = "#F5F5F5", # Missing days get a very faint empty block
    name = params$metric_name
  ) +
  
  theme_cerp() +
  
  # Strip unnecessary axes to leave a pure grid aesthetic
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    legend.key.width = unit(2, "cm"),
    
    # Clean up the axes and grids
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), # Hide week numbers
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    
    # Format the Month headers
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", hjust = 0),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.2, "lines") # Pull the months close together
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = paste("High-Frequency Engagement:", params$metric_name),
    subtitle = "Tracking daily systemic interaction. Darker squares indicate higher adoption rates.",
    caption = "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
  )
```
